office_does: The county sheriff leads law enforcement in unincorporated areas, operates county public-safety functions, and manages the sheriff's office.
race_blurb: Two sheriff-office veterans compete, with Richard Schram presenting the longer command record and Ashley Daschofsky emphasizing proactive intelligence-led policing.

candidates.summary: Chief deputy with more than 23 years in the department and agency-management experience.
highlights: Has command experience across patrol, incidents, training, and agency management [S2][S3]
Emphasizes budgeting, employee development, collaboration, and current constitutional policies [S2]

experience.basis: More than 23 years in the department and current chief-deputy command responsibilities establish a maximal record-based profile.

candidates.summary: County deputy with dispatch, investigation, K9, training, and leadership experience.
highlights: Served in dispatch, collision investigation, K9, field training, and leadership roles [S2][S3]
Emphasizes proactive intelligence-led policing, visibility, accountability, and rural service [S2]

safety.basis: Explicit emphasis on proactive intelligence-led policing, visibility, and officer accountability aligns with the enforcement pole.

experience.basis: Multiple operational and training roles in the sheriff's office establish a substantial record, though not Schram's command tenure.
